questions.questionText: A patient has an unstable sense of self, intense fear of abandonment, impulsive behaviors (self-harm, substance use), chronic feelings of emptiness, and highly unstable relationships alternating between idealization and devaluation. Diagnosis?
questions.optionB: Borderline personality disorder (Cluster B — emotional/dramatic; characterized by instability in self-image, relationships, affect, and marked impulsivity)
questions.explanation: Core concept: BPD = instability in relationships, self-image, affect + impulsivity. Why correct: BPD (Cluster B): abandonment fears, splitting (idealization/devaluation), identity disturbance, impulsivity (self-harm, SUD, reckless behavior), chronic emptiness, affective instability, anger outbursts, transient stress-related paranoid ideation/dissociation. Why A wrong: Antisocial PD (Cluster B) — disregard for rights of others, deceitfulness, impulsivity, aggression, lack of remorse. ≥18 years + conduct disorder before 15. Why C wrong: Narcissistic PD — grandiosity, need for admiration, lack of empathy. Why D wrong: Schizoid PD (Cluster A) — detachment from social relationships, restricted affect, preference for solitude. Clinical relevance: Treatment: DBT (Dialectical Behavior Therapy) = most effective for BPD. No approved medications but symptomatic treatment (mood stabilizers, low-dose antipsychotics). Exam trap: Cluster A = Weird (paranoid, schizoid, schizotypal). Cluster B = Wild (antisocial, borderline, histrionic, narcissistic). Cluster C = Worried (avoidant, dependent, obsessive-compulsive). Memory aid: 'BPD = Borderline = Boundaries blurred in identity, relationships, emotions.' Reference: Ahuja – Personality Disorders.
questions.conceptTag: BPD borderline splitting instability Cluster B DBT
